methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $21M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 8 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $125M - $3.0B.

private.name: NODA AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series A), post-money $125M

data.narrative:
Integrate is a Series A defense and security company that has raised $21M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 10 peers—2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms in the same sector and stage band, including Anduril Industries, Helsing, Shield AI, and Saronic.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses total raised ($21M) as a proxy for current valuation, applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to subsequent-stage advancement, yielding $42M. Peer-median analysis examines 8 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables with valuations ranging from $125M to $3.0B; the median across this set is $1.0B. The identified sample peers—Dream Security ($3.0B), Harmattan AI ($1.4B), Code Metal ($1.2B), Castelion ($1.0B), and Defense Unicorns ($1.0B)—all trade at Series B, suggesting material upside for an earlier-stage entrant.

Synthesizing both methods produces a triangulated range of $36M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median of $521M. The wide dispersion reflects uncertainty inherent in early-stage defense-tech valuations, where peer outcomes span three orders of magnitude. Key sensitivities include Integrate's product maturity relative to Series B peers, government customer concentration and contract visibility, and defensibility of technology differentiation within a crowded frontier-defense landscape.
